description: Funhouse agent integration test scenarios. Each scenario has a sequence of questions with expected engineering checks.

scenarios.title: Bearing Capacity - Strip Footing on Sand

questions.text: Calculate the ultimate and allowable bearing capacity of a 2m wide strip footing embedded 1.5m deep in sand with friction angle 30 degrees and unit weight 18 kN/m3. Use Vesic method with FOS=3.
Now what if the groundwater table is at the surface? How does that change the bearing capacity?

checks.logic: q_ultimate should decrease compared to previous answer

scenarios.title: Bearing Capacity - Square Footing on Clay

questions.text: What is the bearing capacity of a 3m x 3m square footing at 2m depth in clay with cu=50 kPa and unit weight 19 kN/m3? Compare Vesic and Meyerhof methods.
Estimate the immediate elastic settlement of a 3m square footing carrying 200 kPa net pressure on soil with Es=15000 kPa and Poisson ratio 0.3.
Now add a 5m thick normally consolidated clay layer below with Cc=0.3, e0=1.0, and initial effective stress 80 kPa. What is the total consolidation settlement?

Analyze the stability of a 10m high 2H:1V slope in homogeneous clay with c=25 kPa, phi=10 degrees, gamma=18 kN/m3. Use Bishop method with a circular slip surface centered at x=5, y=15 with radius 18m. The ground surface goes from (0,10) to (20,10) to (40,0) to (60,0).
Search for the critical circular slip surface using a grid search. What is the minimum factor of safety?

scenarios.title: Driven Pile Capacity in Layered Soil

questions.text: Calculate the axial capacity of a 15m long, 0.4m diameter closed-end steel pipe pile driven through 5m of soft clay (cu=30 kPa, gamma=17 kN/m3) into 10m of medium dense sand (phi=32, gamma=19 kN/m3). GWT at 2m depth.
Generate a capacity vs depth curve for this pile from 5m to 20m.

checks.logic: capacity should generally increase with depth

questions.text: Classify a site with average shear wave velocity Vs30=250 m/s. What are the site coefficients for Ss=1.0g and S1=0.4g?

checks.logic: Vs30=250 should classify as Site Class D

questions.text: Evaluate liquefaction triggering for a sand layer at 5m depth with N160=12, fines content 15%, unit weight 19 kN/m3, PGA=0.3g, M=7.5, GWT at 2m.

checks.logic: low N160 with high PGA likely liquefiable

questions.text: Check the stability of a 6m high cantilever retaining wall with base width 4m, toe length 1m, stem thickness 0.5m, and base thickness 0.6m. Backfill: phi=32 degrees, gamma=18 kN/m3. Foundation soil: phi=35, gamma=20.
Analyze a 10m deep braced excavation in soft clay (cu=40 kPa, gamma=18 kN/m3) with struts at 3m and 7m depth. Check basal heave stability.

checks.logic: should identify stability concerns in soft clay

questions.text: Generate an HTML calculation package for a bearing capacity analysis of a 2.5m square footing at 1.5m depth in sand with phi=35, gamma=18.5 kN/m3. Project name: 'Highway Bridge B-42', project number 'P2024-001', engineer 'S. O'Connell'. Save to output/bc_test.html.

checks.logic: should produce a real HTML file with formatted calculations

questions.text: Generate a PDF calculation package for settlement analysis of a 4m square footing with 150 kPa applied pressure. Immediate settlement using elastic method with Es=20000 kPa. Project: 'Warehouse Foundation WF-7', number 'P2024-002'. Save to output/settlement_test.pdf.

checks.logic: should produce real PDF via LaTeX (or report LaTeX not available)

questions.text: Create an HTML calc package for slope stability analysis of an 8m high, 2.5H:1V slope in clay with c=30 kPa, phi=12 degrees, gamma=17.5 kN/m3. Use Bishop method. Surface from (0,8) to (20,8) to (40,0) to (60,0). Project: 'Levee Segment LS-3', number 'P2024-003'. Save to output/slope_test.html.

checks.logic: should include cross-section figure in the calc package

questions.text: Create an HTML calc package for a driven pile analysis. 12m long, 0.35m diameter closed-end pipe pile in sand with phi=30, gamma=18 kN/m3. Project: 'Pier Foundation PF-12', number 'P2024-004'. Save to output/pile_test.html.

I need to design a foundation for a column load of 800 kN. The site has medium dense sand with phi=32, gamma=18 kN/m3, and Es=25000 kPa at a depth of 1.5m. First, what size square footing do I need for a factor of safety of 3?

checks.logic: should recommend a footing size that gives FOS>=3 for 800 kN

questions.text: Using that footing size, estimate the immediate settlement. Is it within the 25mm limit?

checks.logic: should use the footing from the previous answer and check against 25mm

questions.text: Now generate an HTML calc package for the bearing capacity analysis with project name 'Column C-14 Foundation' and save to output/foundation_bc.html.

checks.logic: should use same parameters from the earlier bearing capacity analysis

questions.text: Generate a bearing graph for a 15m long steel pipe pile (area 0.012 m2) using a Delmag D30-32 hammer. Skin friction is 50% of total capacity. Show resistance from 200 to 2000 kN.

checks.logic: blow count should increase with resistance

questions.text: I have a site with soft clay (fines content 80%) that needs improvement to 8m depth. What ground improvement methods are feasible? Then analyze wick drain consolidation with ch=2.5 m2/yr at 1.5m triangular spacing over 6 months.

checks.logic: should recommend PVDs for soft clay; consolidation degree should be partial at 6 months
